executable.title: Curvature Anisotropic Diffusion Filter
executable.description: Applies curvature-based anisotropic diffusion to a 3D NIFTI image. Smooths along iso-intensity contours (level sets) rather than across them, which tends to produce cartoon-like smoothing with sharper flat regions. Controlled by conductance, timeStep, and iterations. ECE:5490 HW04 — Partho Ghosh
executable.acknowledgements: Developed as part of ECE:5490 Multi-Dimensional Image Analysis Tools, Spring 2026, University of Iowa. Course instructor: Professor Hans Johnson. This work uses the Insight Toolkit (ITK), an open-source library for medical image analysis developed by the National Library of Medicine (NLM) and the Insight Software Consortium (ISC). Slicer Execution Model (SEM) developed by Kitware Inc. and the Slicer community (slicer.org). For understanding and help with the coding and writing Claude.ai has been used.

double.name: conductance
double.description: Conductance parameter. Higher values allow more diffusion. Typical range: 0.5–5.0.
double.label: Conductance

double.name: timeStep
double.description: Time step per iteration. Must be small for numerical stability (typically 0.0625 for 3D).
double.label: Time Step

image.name: outputVolume
image.label: Output Volume
image.description: Curvature anisotropic diffusion filtered output NIFTI image.
